description: Need to set up a basic Express server at /home/user/api — just a single endpoint GET /health that returns `{"status":"ok"}` with content-type application/json. Port 3000. Nothing fancy, just need it runnable with `node server.js`.
truth:
Initial state:
- /home/user/api directory exists and is writable.
- /home/user/api/package.json exists with: `{"name": "api", "version": "1.0.0", "main": "server.js"}`
- express is NOT installed yet — no node_modules directory.
- Node.js 20.x and npm are available.
- /home/user/api/server.js does not exist.

Expected final state:
- /home/user/api/server.js exists.
- /home/user/api/node_modules/express exists (express installed).
- Running `cd /home/user/api && node server.js &` starts the server, then `curl -s http://localhost:3000/health` returns exactly `{"status":"ok"}` (whitespace-insensitive JSON equivalence acceptable).
- `curl -sI http://localhost:3000/health | grep -i content-type` contains `application/json`.
- Server process can be started and responds on port 3000.

Invariants:
- /home/user/api/package.json still exists (may have dependencies added).
- No other ports required.

Anti-shortcut guards:
- server.js must use express (or at minimum node's http module) — `grep -E 'require\s*\(\s*["\x27]express["\x27]\s*\)|from\s+["\x27]express["\x27]|require\s*\(\s*["\x27]http["\x27]\s*\)|from\s+["\x27]http["\x27]' /home/user/api/server.js` should match at least one.
- The /health endpoint must actually be implemented in code, not just return a static file.
name: task_000000_0a7ca2a5
